pr.title: Add DevCom struct, match JObjSortAnim, fix iref_INC calls involving JObj
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: PR 1362 updated SysDolphin baselib typing and matching around DevCom callbacks, JObj animation/refcount handling, and RObj JObj references. It introduced an HSD_DevCom struct and ARQRequest storage, corrected HSD_DevComCallback and lbDvd/lbFile callback signatures from int placeholders to void*/bool-shaped parameters, matched or improved JObjSortAnim and related JObj animation traversal code, and replaced ad hoc JObj-specific ref/iref helpers with shared object/JObj wrapper APIs. The diff also normalized several HSD_ASSERT line constants from hex to decimal. There were no review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Added typedef and definition for struct HSD_DevCom in src/sysdolphin/baselib/devcom.h, with next, dcReq, file, src, dest, size, type, cancelflag, callback, and args fields.
Added declaration for HSD_DevComUnlink(HSD_DevCom* dc, int link, HSD_DevCom* adc).
Added #include <dolphin/ar/arq.h> and global ARQRequest devComARQR[2][2] in devcom.c.
Changed HSD_DevComCallback in forward.h from typedef void (*)(int, int, int, bool) to typedef void (*)(void*, int, void*, bool).
Updated lbDvd_80017E64 and lbFile_8001615C signatures and headers to use void* for the first and third callback-style parameters and bool cancelflag for the fourth parameter.
Rewrote JObjSortAnim loop as a for-loop over HSD_FObj** fobj_ptr, preserving the behavior of moving the first TYPE_JOBJ fobj to the head of aobj->fobj.
Simplified HSD_JObjAddAnim by replacing a local has_dobj flag derived from JOBJ_PTCL/JOBJ_SPLINE with union_type_dobj(jobj).

Renamed HSD_JObjAddAnimAll parameters and locals from register-style names such as var_r31/var_r26 to semantic names jp, aj, mj, sj, ajoint, mjoint, and sjoint, while preserving recursive traversal of child JObjs and animation-joint siblings.

Removed local JObj-only ref_INC_alt/jobj_iref_INC helpers and string literals used to support inline asm-like matching, replacing calls with HSD_JObjRef, HSD_JObjRefThis, and shared iref_INC/ref_INC helpers.
Changed HSD_JObjResolveRefs to assert jobj->child with HSD_ASSERT(1108, jobj->child) and then call HSD_JObjRef(jobj->child) instead of a local ref_INC alias.
Changed HSD_JObjUnref to call shared iref_INC(jobj) rather than a local jobj_iref_INC helper.
Changed HSD_JObjSetCurrent to call HSD_JObjRef(jobj) instead of ref_INC(jobj).
Changed HSD_JObjRefThis in jobj.h to guard against NULL before calling iref_INC.

Reordered and adjusted object.h inline refcount helpers: ref_DEC and ref_INC appear before ref_CNT/iref helpers; ref_INC now uses HSD_ASSERT(93, ...); iref_INC no longer checks NULL internally and directly increments ref_count_individual then asserts nonzero.
Updated RObj JObj reference paths in HSD_RObjResolveRefs, HSD_RvalueResolveRefs, and HSD_RObjSetConstraintObj to use HSD_JObjRefThis instead of directly calling iref_INC.

Inlined the former robj.c ref_JObj helper into HSD_RObjSetConstraintObj while preserving the hsdObjIsDescendantOf check against hsdJObj.parent.parent and the 'constraint only support jobj target.' assert path.
Converted several HSD_ASSERT/HSD_Panic line-number arguments in jobj.c from hex constants to decimal equivalents, such as 0x3CC to 972 and 0x7D3 to 2003.
smart_moves: Centralized JObj individual-reference increments through HSD_JObjRefThis instead of direct iref_INC calls in RObj code, making JObj-specific NULL behavior explicit at the wrapper level after iref_INC was made non-null-tolerant.
Removed duplicate local refcount helper implementations from jobj.c, reducing the risk that local copies generate subtly different asserts, string literals, or codegen from object.h/JObj APIs.

Used union_type_dobj(jobj) in HSD_JObjAddAnim rather than manually duplicating the JOBJ_PTCL/JOBJ_SPLINE exclusion logic, improving semantic clarity and likely helping matching by using an existing predicate.
Improved DevCom callback typing all the way through forward.h, lbDvd.h/lbdvd.c, and lbfile.h/lbfile.c instead of only changing one callsite, keeping callback prototypes consistent.

Kept HSD_RObjSetConstraintObj's descendant validation in the final function while removing a small wrapper helper, preserving safety diagnostics and avoiding an extra helper that may have complicated matching.
Used a pointer-to-pointer traversal in JObjSortAnim, a common C tactic for unlinking/relinking list nodes without needing a previous pointer.
decomp_lessons: When a shared inline such as iref_INC changes NULL-handling semantics, audit all direct callers and route object-family-specific cases through typed wrappers such as HSD_JObjRefThis.

Callback signatures should reflect observed ABI semantics even when symbol names are still unknown: replacing int placeholders with void* and bool can clarify DevCom/lbDvd/lbFile relationships without requiring full naming of all parameters.
For matching, prefer existing semantic helpers like union_type_dobj over temporary boolean locals when the helper matches the original branching shape or reduces artificial stack/register pressure.
Local duplicate inlines may be useful temporarily, but once common helpers can match they should be removed to reduce string literal clutter and keep assertions anchored in the canonical header.
Linked-list reordering functions can often be matched cleanly with a pointer-to-pointer for-loop; this pattern avoids extra prev variables and naturally supports moving a node to the list head.
If a low-level helper intentionally assumes non-NULL input for codegen, put NULL guards in higher-level typed wrappers rather than in every callsite.
Struct introduction can unlock better signatures elsewhere: defining HSD_DevCom gave context for HSD_DevComCallback, HSD_DevComUnlink, and DevCom ARQ state.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_DevCom for the DevCom request struct and typedef the struct name before defining it.
Use HSD_DevComCallback for callbacks with signature void* key/arg-like parameter, int index, void* value/arg-like parameter, bool cancelflag.
Use semantic traversal local names in JObj animation code: jp for HSD_JObj*, aj for HSD_AnimJoint*, mj for HSD_MatAnimJoint*, and sj for HSD_ShapeAnimJoint*.
Use ajoint, mjoint, and sjoint as HSD_JObjAddAnimAll parameter names instead of generic arg1/arg2/arg3.
Use HSD_JObjRef and HSD_JObjRefThis for JObj references rather than raw ref_INC or iref_INC at JObj-related callsites.
The diff uses decimal source line numbers in HSD_ASSERT/HSD_Panic calls in touched JObj code rather than hex literals.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: JObjSortAnim was matched or improved by changing a while loop into a for-loop over fobj_ptr = &aobj->fobj with fobj_ptr = &fobj->next as the increment expression.
HSD_JObjAddAnim removed a stack/local bool has_dobj and directly used union_type_dobj(jobj), likely reducing stack/register noise.
HSD_JObjAddAnimAll removed a todo saying it should match once HSD_JObjAddAnim matches, suggesting the HSD_JObjAddAnim cleanup enabled or contributed to matching the recursive AddAnimAll routine.
object.h ref_INC now uses HSD_ASSERT instead of manually calling __assert with the literal "object.h", allowing jobj.c to delete local string-literal workarounds such as HSD_JObj_804068E4.
Making iref_INC non-null-guarded in object.h paired with NULL guards in HSD_JObjRefThis likely matched callsites where the original code assumed a valid object after an assert or ID lookup.
Inlining ref_JObj into HSD_RObjSetConstraintObj may have adjusted register allocation/control flow while keeping the same descendant-check logic.
Several assert line constants were converted from hex to decimal, which can matter for macro expansion or matching consistency when comparing generated constants.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that further define HSD_DevCom fields or implement/decompile HSD_DevComRequest and HSD_DevComUnlink.
Search for remaining direct iref_INC calls involving HSD_JObj or RObj constraints to ensure they use HSD_JObjRefThis when NULL-safety or JObj semantics are needed.
Check whether other HSD_DevComCallback implementers still use stale int,int,int,bool signatures.
Search for remaining local duplicate ref_INC or iref_INC helpers in baselib source files.
Investigate subsequent matching work on HSD_JObjRemoveAll, which still has a todo for regswaps in this diff excerpt.
